source_jobs.title: Business Operations
source_jobs.description:
WHAT ARE WE DOING?

Medra’s mission is to eradicate disease.




WHAT WE'RE BUILDING:

We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:
🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity.
🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.




THE TEAM:

 - We’re a team of passionate, mission-driven people from companies like Tesla, Applied Intuition, SpaceX, Neuralink, Scale, Samsara, and Vercel. We’re collaborative and committed to moving fast with extreme prioritization.

 - Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don’t mind intense discussions about tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically. Feedback of all types is not just welcome, but expected.

 - As a team, we’re always looking for ways to grow and improve. We love learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.




BIZOPS AT MEDRA:

This is a 0→1 role at the center of a company scaling fast. This is not a support role. You are the operational infrastructure the team runs on. Every system you build, every workflow you automate, every fire you put out makes the entire company faster. When something falls through the cracks, you're the person who notices, owns it, and ships a fix before anyone has to ask.

We're a startup doing the work of a company five times our size. There are no established playbooks. If you're looking for defined processes and clear swim lanes, this isn't the right fit. If you see a room full of broken workflows, missing systems, and a half-built CRM and your first instinct is to start fixing things, keep reading.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Business Operations: Own day-to-day business operations and keep the company running while it scales.

 - Process Improvement: Dig into cross-functional pain points, find root causes, and fix the underlying systems, not just the symptoms. You'll touch finance, hiring, customer success, and ops, often in the same week.

 - Go-to-Market and Sales Support: Step into sales and GTM on short notice. Pitch decks, prospect pipelines, outbound messaging, collateral. Whatever the Sales/GTM/BD team needs to move faster.

 - Automation and Systems: Build automations and kill manual work. You're AI-native and you treat every repetitive process as a problem to solve.

 - Special Projects: Take on special projects that are high-value, loosely scoped, and need someone who can figure it out without being told how.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 1-3 years in consulting, finance, product, or business development, or you're early-career with a steep slope and a track record of shipping real work.

 - You do what you say you're going to do, on time, every time. No task is beneath you and no problem is someone else's.

 - You don't wait for structure. You create it. You've built a process, a tool, or a system from nothing because you saw the gap and couldn't leave it alone.

 - You operate fast. You'd rather ship something 80% right today and iterate than spend a week perfecting a plan.

 - Direct communicator. You take feedback without flinching and give it without hedging.


BONUS POINTS FOR:

 - You've worked in ops at a high-growth startup and know what "scaling while everything is on fire" actually feels like.

 - Background in math, statistics, engineering, or science.

 - You've built internal knowledge bases or automations that saved your team real hours every week.

This position is 100% in-person, you must be able to come on-site to our San Francisco office. We offer relocation benefits for applicants who need to relocate.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - An opportunity to fundamentally change the way that scientific research happens

 - Fast-paced, creative, and collaborative work environment

 - Sig

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING:

At Medra, our mission is to use AI, robotics, and biology to accelerate life science research, with the ultimate goal of eradicating disease. We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:

🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity.

🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.




THE TEAM:

 - We're a team of passionate, mission-driven engineers and scientists from companies like Tesla, Amazon, SpaceX, and Neuralink. We're collaborative and love moving fast, both with our product and on team trips skiing or go-karting!

 - As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

 - Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don't mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.


THE ROLE:

We are looking for a Mechanical Engineer to help make our vision a reality. As one of our first hires you will work very closely to build a flexible and reliable robotic automation solution. You will be responsible for designing, testing and implementing the hardware that our robots need to fully automate customer protocols. A love for operating in an experimental and fast paced environment is a must.

In this role, you will:

 - Analyze, test, validate and iterate on mechanical systems

 - Create detailed designs in CAD and well-specified, accurate drawings

 - Design, fabricate, test and iterate on hardware prototypes

 - Develop robotic solutions to manipulate and control biology tools designed for humans

Let's talk if you have:

 - Strong mechanical intuition and hardware debugging and assembly skills

 - Passion for the gritty details of part design, material selection, and manufacturing techniques

 - Experience with mechanical engineering tools and best practices in production to design components, produce drawings, and build assemblies

 - Experience designing, building, testing, and scaling hardware

 - A hacker’s mindset - getting quickly to a good-enough solution

 - Are hands-on and willing to cross discipline boundaries to solve a problem

 - Know your way around rapid prototyping technology

 - A willingness to wear many hats / a flexible attitude towards responsibilities and scope

 - BS/MS in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics or a related discipline, or equivalent experience

(OPTIONAL) Bonus points if you have:

 - Experience managing PLM/document control systems

 - Mechatronics and circuit design background

 - Knowledge of robotics, motion control, camera, force/torque sensors

 - Experience or familiarity with robot arm manipulators

 - Experience with robotics in a laboratory or manufacturing environment

 - Experience writing code for robotics systems

What we offer:

 - 401k

 - Medical insurance

 - Dental Insurance

 - Relocation benefits

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Weekday dinners

This position is 100% in-person, you must be able to come on-site to our San Francisco office. We offer relocation benefits for applicants who need to relocate.

Medra is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion or religious creed, mental or physical disability, medical condition, genetic information, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, and related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, military or veteran status, citizenship, or other characteristics protected by state, federal or local law or our other policies. We value diversity at Medra a

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.

 - As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

 - Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don’t mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.


IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own the customer relationship from pre-sale through post-sale, deployment, & expansion

 - Embed with customer teams to assess key pain points, understand scientific workflows, & identify critical requirements

 - Translate business objectives into a clear roadmap for deployment at our customers, mapping out milestones, dependencies, and success criteria

 - Dynamically move between high-level strategy and execution-level detail to plan and understand how day-to-day engineering activities map to overall goals, make complex technical trade-offs, and proactively forecast roadblocks

 - Operate with extreme ownership and set the pace for the team to ensure we ship on time

 - Design, track, and optimize customer success metrics to build the business case

 - Build deep relationships with executive sponsors and act as a strategic thought partner on their science and AI strategy

 - Develop scalable playbooks for customer engagement and integrate feedback into product development efforts


LET'S TALK IF YOU HAVE:

 - Technical background in Bioengineering / Biological Engineering / Chemical Engineering / Computational Biology / Biological Sciences / related field

 - 3+ years customer-facing experience in management or strategy consulting, BD in biopharma / biotech, technical deployments, or other strategic roles

 - 3+ years of experience working in a biological research lab, across academia or industry, and technical fluency of scientific wet lab workflows

 - A deep interest in the life sciences and a demonstrated eagerness to learn quickly

 - Track record of successfully leading complex, high-stakes customer engagements that relied on fast decision-making and tight coordination internally and externally

 - Experience thriving in cross-functional collaboration, effectively working with teams across Engineering, Product, & Sales

 - Customer obsession and a passion for deeply understanding user needs and delivering impactful solutions

 - A bias for action, solving immediate customer challenges while also focusing on scalable, repeatable processes

 - Strong communicator with executive presence & relationship-building capability,

 - Exceptional ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value

 - Excel in ambiguous environments where there is minimal structure and no playbook

 - Low-ego, high agency, team-first, and first-principles mindset


BONUS POINTS FOR:

 - Past experience working at early-stage, high-growth startups.

 - Past experience working with robotics, hardware, or software engineers.



This position is 100% in-person, you must be able to come on-site to our San Francisco office. We offer relocation benefits for applicants who need to relocate. Travel is required, up to 25-50%.

source_jobs.title: Office Manager
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Francisco, CA

Workplace: Onsite, 5 days per week



What We’re Building:

At Medra, our mission is to use AI, robotics, and biology to accelerate life science research, with the ultimate goal of eradicating disease. We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:

🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity.

🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.


The Team:

• We're a team of passionate, mission-driven engineers and scientists from companies like Tesla, Amazon, SpaceX, and Neuralink. We're collaborative and love moving fast, both with our product and on team trips skiing or go-karting!

• As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

• Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don't mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.



The Role:

We’re looking for a highly organized, scrappy, and people-focused Office Manager to own the day-to-day operations of our San Francisco office. This person will be the backbone of the workplace experience: keeping the office running smoothly, supporting employees onsite, coordinating vendors and facilities, managing meals and snacks, and helping bring a new space to life as the company grows.

This person will play a critical role in shaping the employee experience and helping our office scale thoughtfully as we grow. We’re looking for someone who can bring order, energy, and ownership to the workplace and create an environment where the team can do their best work.



In This Role, You Will:

 - Own daily office operations for our San Francisco office

 - Partner with a designer to help set up and operationalize a new office space

 - Manage facilities-related needs, including maintenance requests, office upkeep, and landlord coordination

 - Serve as the main point of contact for building issues such as HVAC, access, repairs, and other workplace needs

 - Manage vendor relationships across office services, supplies, meals, maintenance, and related partners

 - Coordinate employee onboarding logistics, including workspace readiness, access setup, and general day-one experience

 - Support basic IT and workplace technology coordination, including office devices, access systems, and troubleshooting with external partners as needed

 - Administer office systems and tools such as badge access and Ring

 - Oversee meal ordering, snack programs, and kitchen stocking

 - Handle mail, deliveries, and package receiving

 - Open doors for visitors, deliveries, and service providers as needed

 - Maintain a clean, organized, and welcoming office environment

 - Anticipate operational gaps and solve problems quickly with minimal direction



Let’s Talk If You Have:

 - Experience in an Office Manager or similar workplace/office operations role

 - Strong organizational skills and excellent attention to detail

 - Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and keep things moving in a high-velocity environment

 - Highly autonomous and resourceful; able to identify what needs to happen and make it happen

 - Strong project management instincts and follow-through

 - Comfortable being hands-on with day-to-day office needs

 - Clear communicator with strong interpersonal skills

 - Positive, service-oriented mindset with a willingness to jump in wherever needed

 - Handy, practical, and scrappy approach to problem-solving



(OPTIONAL) Bonus Points If You Have:

 - Experience with facilities coordination

 - Experience planning office events or team gatherings

 - Ex

source_jobs.title: Scientist, Cell Biology & Antibody Engineering
source_jobs.description:
What We’re Building:

At Medra, our mission is to use AI, robotics, and biology to accelerate life science research, with the ultimate goal of eradicating disease. We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:

🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity.

🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.


The Team:

• We're a team of passionate, mission-driven engineers and scientists from companies like Tesla, Amazon, SpaceX, and Neuralink. We're collaborative and love moving fast, both with our product and on team trips skiing or go-karting!

• As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

• Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don't mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.



The Role:

We are looking for a Scientist to join our team and to develop and execute antibody screening and characterization on the Medra Physical AI platform. You will support our tissue culture operations, design and run in vitro and cell-based assays to characterize antibody candidates, and contribute to building out the lab capabilities that make all of this possible — from new assay formats to the automated workflows that execute them at scale.

This is a role for someone who blends scientific judgment with hands-on craft — someone who has designed and run antibody characterization and functional assays in high stakes settings and is energized by the idea of translating bench expertise into an autonomous lab. You will work closely with scientists and roboticists to develop and validate assays and bring them into routine execution on our platform.



In This Role, You Will:

 - Design, execute, and interpret antibody screening and characterization campaigns across the full funnel — from high-throughput primary hit triage through orthogonal confirmation

 - Develop and run cell-based binding and functional assays using flow cytometry to characterize antibodies, VHHs, and other binder formats

 - Run biophysical characterization on antibody candidates — including SPR and BLI for affinity and kinetics, and epitope binning — and interpret results to inform go/no-go

 - Execute developability assays to assess manufacturability and liability risks early — including thermal stability (DSF/DSC), aggregation and monomericity (HPLC-SEC), hydrophobicity (HPLC-HIC), polyspecificity/non-specific binding, and expression titer — and help triage candidates based on integrated characterization data

 - Maintain and engineer mammalian cell lines (e.g., Expi293, ExpiCHO, HEK293, CHO) and establish cell-based characterization and functional assays

 - Build out new lab capabilities end-to-end: scope the need, evaluate technologies, qualify instruments, write SOPs, and prepare validated workflows for autonomous execution

 - Partner with roboticists and software engineers to translate manual assay workflows into reliable, high-throughput autonomous protocols on the Medra platform

 - Maintain rigorous experimental records in electronic lab notebooks (ELN) and communicate results clearly to internal and external stakeholders



Let’s Talk If You Have:

 - Ph.D. in cell biology, immunology, biochemistry, or a related discipline with 2+ years of industry experience, or a B.S./M.S. with 6+ years of deep, hands-on industry experience.

 - Strong background in mammalian cell culture and sterile technique, with a track record of maintaining healthy, reproducible cell lines for assay work

 - Deep hands-on expertise in flow cytometry, including high-thr

At Medra, our mission is to use AI, robotics, and biology to accelerate life science research, with the ultimate goal of eradicating disease. We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:

🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity.

🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.




THE TEAM:

 - We're a team of passionate, mission-driven engineers and scientists from companies like Tesla, Amazon, SpaceX, and Neuralink. We're collaborative and love moving fast, both with our product and on team trips skiing or go-karting!

 - As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

 - Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don't mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.


IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own the architecture and implementation of full-stack systems end-to-end — writing code daily and being in the critical path on the hardest problems, not delegating them.

 - Design and implement user experiences that spark joy in our customers.

 - Build kick-ass internal tools to allow us to build, support and scale our robotic fleet across multiple customer deployments.

 - Set the technical direction and quality bar for full-stack engineering, and make the hard tradeoff calls on design, scope, and prioritization.

 - Grow a small team of talented full-stack engineers — hiring, mentoring, and unblocking them as a force multiplier alongside your own technical work.


LET'S TALK IF YOU HAVE:

 - Deep proficiency with Python and the ability to rapidly implement and iterate on ideas.

 - Experience building interactive front-ends with React or similar.

 - Experience with TypeScript.

 - Understanding of programming for the web (HTML, CSS, JS, HTTP, REST).

 - Significant backend system design and implementation experience.

 - A track record of being the engineer your team relies on to ship the hardest things.

 - Willingness to wear many hats / a flexible attitude towards responsibilities and scope.

 - Ability to move fast in an environment where things are sometimes loosely defined and you may have competing priorities or deadlines.

 - Passion for learning and expanding your skillset.

 - Experience leading or mentoring software engineers — and a desire to keep doing that while remaining hands-on technically.


(OPTIONAL) BONUS POINTS IF YOU HAVE:

 - Experience with database technologies like Postgres and Redis.

 - Interest and experience in robotics, machine learning, or computer vision (bonus points for past experience working with hardware).


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - An opportunity to change the way that scientific research happens

 - Fast-paced, creative, and collaborative work environment

 - Significant equity ownership

 - 401k

 - Medical insurance and dental insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Weekday dinners

This position is 100% in-person, you must be able to come on-site to our San Francisco office. We offer relocation benefits for applicants who need to relocate.

source_jobs.title: Research Associate - Protein
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING

At Medra, our mission is to use AI, robotics, and biology to accelerate life science research, with the ultimate goal of eradicating disease. We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:

🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity. 🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a highly hands-on Research Associate to join our team and support protein science and cell biology workflows on the Medra Physical AI platform. This is a role for someone who loves being at the bench — someone who has spent years honing their craft in a fast-paced industry lab and takes pride in rigorous, reproducible science.

You will work closely with scientists and roboticists to execute, validate, and optimize protein characterization workflows and cell-based assays as part of our autonomous lab platform. You thrive when given a specific task to own end-to-end, and you bring the experience and intuition to operate independently.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Execute protein expression experiments across multiple host systems (e.g., mammalian, E. coli, cell-free) at various scales, from small-scale screens to larger production runs

 - Run protein characterization assays, including SPR, BLI, DSF/DSC, and various developability assays.

 - Perform cell-based binding and functional assays to evaluate candidates against cell-surface targets and assess functional activity.

 - Own day-to-day operation and organization of the cell culture facility, including routine passaging using sterile technique, inventory management, and equipment maintenance.

 - Maintain accurate and detailed experimental records in electronic lab notebooks (ELN), and contribute to SOP development

 - Collaborate with scientists and cross-functional team members to ensure smooth handoffs and timely execution across the protein screening and characterization workflow.




WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

 - B.S. or B.A., or MS in Biology, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, or a related scientific discipline

 - 2-4 years (M.S.) or 4–6 (B.S./B.A. )years of hands-on, industry lab experience in protein science — we value deep bench experience over advanced degrees

 - Demonstrated expertise in protein expression using mammalian cell lines (e.g., Expi293, ExpiCHO) and/or microbial systems

 - Strong hands-on experience with high-throughput protein quality control and biophysical characterization.

 - Experience maintaining mammalian cell lines and performing cell-based assays in an industry setting, including flow cytometry.

 - Strong documentation habits, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently on defined tasks

 - A scrappy, can-do attitude — you figure things out and get things done




BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Familiarity with developability assessment assays (HIC, AC-SINS, PSR, or similar)

 - Familiarity with analytical characterization techniques such as HPLC-SEC, HPLC-HIC, or FACS-based binding assays

 - Familiarity with imaging-based cell assays (e.g., high-content imaging, fluorescence microscopy)

 - Experience with cell-free protein expression or specialized enzyme workflows

 - Comfort working with lab automation or robotics platforms



This position is 100% in-person; you must be able to come on-site to our San Francisco office. We offer relocation benefits for applicants who need to relocate.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - An opportunity to change the way that scientific research happens

 - Fast-paced, creative, and collaborative work environment

 - Significant equity ownership

 - 401k

 - Medical and dental insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Weekday dinners

source_jobs.title: Scientist, Biology
source_jobs.description:
What We’re Building:

At Medra, our mission is to use AI, robotics, and biology to accelerate life science research, with the ultimate goal of eradicating disease. We have been quietly building the foundational layers of our Physical AI Scientist platform:

🤖 Physical AI that can operate scientific instruments with human-level dexterity.

🧪 Scientific AI that can analyze results, reason about next steps, and close the loop autonomously.

This is an incredibly ambitious mission, but we believe that a team of ambitious people with high ownership can accomplish incredible things.



The Team:

• We're a team of passionate, mission-driven engineers and scientists from companies like Tesla, Amazon, SpaceX, and Neuralink. We're collaborative and love moving fast, both with our product and on team trips skiing or go-karting!

• As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

• Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don't mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.



The Role:

As a scientist on our forward-deployed team, your objective is to embed yourself with your scientific counterparts at the customer, acting as a true partner on their team to ensure that the Medra platform is achieving scientific outcomes and driving value for the organization. You will become an expert in our customer’s science, deeply understanding their scientific requirements and applying the scientific method (hypothesis, design, experimentation, analysis) to test and validate the fidelity of our Physical AI Scientist platform for their workflows. You will be in close communication with our end users (scientists) and have your eyes and ears on the ground to help us get ahead of any issues, while taking any insights and learnings from the customer back to the company to guide improvements to our product.

Internally, you will partner closely with the engagement manager and forward-deployed engineers to drive a successful deployment. You will work alongside an elite team of robotics, software, and hardware engineers to provide scientific oversight during scoping, build, and deployment. You will be a platform super-user who understands the strengths and limitations of our platform, and can apply creativity (& scientific intuition) to solve challenging problems.

We’re looking for someone who acts with urgency and thrives under pressure. This is a unique opportunity to use your skillset to what will be a step-change in drug discovery through artificial intelligence and robotics, and will offer significantly more exposure than working in a traditional biotech or pharma role.



In This Role, You Will:

 - Be the scientific subject matter expert - understand customer requirements and provide biological context to guide scoping, product development, and capability prioritization.

 - Design & execute wet-lab experiments to validate Medra’s autonomous Physical AI against scientific KPIs/metrics

 - Deploy on-site to our customer’s labs - embed with their scientists to validate the science, conduct hands-on training on the robot & instruments, and provide ongoing support as they ramp up usage

 - Own the installation, QC, and troubleshooting/error-handing for all laboratory instruments on the Physical AI platform

 - Support internal R&D on new protocols and assays, and explore emerging technologies & techniques

 - Keep detailed experimental records and documentation (SOPs) for all workflows

 - Communicate results clearly, succinctly, and on-time



Let’s Talk If You Have:

 - Master's degree + 3 years of experience OR PhD in biological sciences or related field

 - Deep scientific expertise as evidenced by a track record of top-tier publications / presentations

 - Excellent interpersonal skills and a demonstrated abili

The Team:

• We're a team of passionate, mission-driven engineers and scientists from companies like Tesla, Amazon, SpaceX, and Neuralink. We're collaborative and love moving fast, both with our product and on team trips skiing or go-karting!

• As a team, we love nerding out about engineering and robotics — plus other topics like race cars or cooking. We like learning new things and then sharing our new knowledge with each other.

• Our team is opinionated and straightforward. We don't mind intense discussions about design tradeoffs. If we have arguments or miscommunication, we resolve conflicts quickly and empathetically.



The Role:

The Scientific Operations role is a highly operational role responsible for the performance, readiness, and continuous improvement of ML001. This person brings strong program management instincts to a scientific environment — owning lab readiness, asset management, inventory, safety and compliance, and facilities coordination. Scientific Operations works closely with scientists, engineers, and the operations team to ensure ML001 runs as a high-utilization, audit-ready facility.



In This Role, You Will:

 - Manage day-to-day lab operations to ensure instruments, consumables, and lab spaces are ready to support scheduled work

 - Own the preventive maintenance and calibration program across all lab equipment — building the system, coordinating vendors, and ensuring record integrity

 - Design and operate a scalable lab inventory program, including par levels, reorder triggers, audit cadence, and SKU-level vendor management

 - Own the lab asset register and lifecycle planning, including warranty tracking and service contract management

 - Develop, implement, and maintain SOPs and lab protocols

 - Manage lab-level facilities work, including HVAC, cold storage, BSL-2 buildout, and equipment layout, in coordination with external facilities vendors

 - Continuously assess workflow and resource utilization, identifying bottlenecks and recommending improvements

 - Build and run the onboarding program for scientists, research associates, and engineers on lab logistics, safety, and operating norms

 - Partner with the operations and procurement teams on capital equipment requests, vendor contracts, and service agreements

 - Partner with science and engineering teams on workflow design, instrument requirements, and integration with automation systems



Let’s Talk If You Have:

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in biology, biochemistry, or a relevant scientific discipline

 - Proven track record of operational or program excellence — whether through management consulting, building systems and processes in a fast-paced environment, or standing up or managing a biology laboratory

 - Familiarity with wet lab environments or a strong ability to quickly develop scientific context on the job

 - Demonstrated experience running a BSL-2 program

 - Track record of building and maintaining SOPs, calibration and PM programs, and inventory systems

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Excellent written and verbal communication



Bonus Points If You Have:

 - Prior experience as a bench scientist

 - Experience supporting multi-customer or contract research environments

 - Experience scaling a lab through a buildout or growth phase

 - LIMS or ELN implementation experience

 - Familiarity with lab automation a

notes: High-tech startup with AI, robotics, and biology focus. Strong engineering core.
